blocks.text: DTC P0966: Driven Pulley Control SOL.V. circuit Short circuit or disconnection

The Driven Pulley Control SOL.V. controls the hydraulic pressure in the hydraulic circuit. A spool in the Driven Pulley Control SOL.V. pushes a valve in response to a duty (load) output that is converted to a current value by the PGM-FI ECU. As a result, the valve pushed by the spool releases oil pressure proportional to the current value. the PGM-FI ECU measures the current flowing in the linear solenoid of the driven pulley control SOL.V. and compares it with the commanded current of the duty output, and the resulting difference is controlled as feedback. the PGM-FI ECU control signals for the driven pulley oil pressure in order to shift gears appropriately. Failure is caused by disconnection or temporary disconnection. When the drive circuit of the driven pulley control SOL.V. is turned on, if the current does not flow to the driven pulley control SOL.V. for a predetermined period of time, the PGM-FI ECU determines a fault and stores a DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status

rows.text: 24-hour (e.g. observation, operation (of a machine), care (of patient))
Detection order (of the DTC in question)
1D/C (single detection method), PGM-FI warning light: off, D indicator light: flashing
Normal judgment, failure judgment, not completed
DTC requesting the prohibition of detection of the relevant DTC

blocks.text: When the drive circuit of the driven pulley control SOL.V. is turned on and no current flows to the driven pulley control SOL.V. for more than 1 second.

items.text: Disconnection (including disconnected or loose coupler) of the cord (DNLS line) between the PGM-FI ECU and the driven pulley control SOL.V.
Driven Pulley Control SOL.V. Internal circuit disconnection

blocks.text: Start the engine and wait at least 1 second at standstill.
If the vehicle is determined to be faulty, the D indicator light flashes without the PGM-FI warning light illuminating, and the temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory.
Use of the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or removal of the 12V battery terminal will clear the D indicator light, temporary DTC, DTC, and freeze data.
